text.title: User Story Mapping Antipatterns
text.summary: Structural and agentic failure modes in user story mapping, with concrete mitigations.

antipattern.description: Activities expressed as feature names ("Search", "Checkout", "Profile") instead of verb-phrases in user voice ("Find a product", "Complete purchase", "Manage account settings").
antipattern.reason: Noun-names produce a 2D Jira view. Verb-phrases reveal journey sequence, user goals, and gaps. The activity-extractor must be constrained to reject noun-only activities.

antipattern.description: Vertical priority dimension collapses to ranks 1, 2, 3 with no forcing function. Everything ends up in R1.
antipattern.reason: Without a release line tied to capacity (story-points sum / velocity × sprints <= 1.0), priority is aspirational. The release-slicer must compute and enforce the capacity constraint.

antipattern.description: Multi-persona stories merged into one map from the start ("shoppers + admins" in the same backbone).
antipattern.reason: Merged maps produce one-size-fits-none stories. Different personas have different journey sequences and different definitions of "essential". Always start per-persona; merge only after each is stable.

antipattern.description: Walking skeleton inflation: R1 padded with "delight" stories because they are "already built".
antipattern.reason: Walking skeleton = minimum to validate the outcome metric. Every non-essential R1 item must provide a removal justification. The opus slicer must request this justification when adding stories beyond the minimum.

antipattern.description: Workshop produces 200 stickies; nobody transcribes them within 24 hours. Map exists only on a wall or whiteboard.
antipattern.reason: Untranscribed workshops are effectively lost within days. Digitize to map.yaml within 24 hours; the workshop is prep for the canonical artifact, not the artifact itself.

antipattern.description: Story-rewriter emits 7 acceptance-criteria scenarios for a 1-point story.
antipattern.reason: AC inflation bloats test-plan.md and slows CI. Cap at 3 GWT scenarios per story; additional scenarios go to a nice_to_have_ac bucket for later.

antipattern.description: Backbone activities reordered by agents on every run, causing downstream task and story churn.
antipattern.reason: Pin backbone order as immutable for the cycle. Require explicit unfreeze flag to reorder. Agents must reject re-orderings that were not explicitly requested.
